headerSubtitle: D.M.A.  |  U.S. Army Veteran (20 Years)  |  Active Federal Secret Clearance
summary: Retired 20-year U.S. Army veteran and Washington Vets-to-Tech graduate moving into cloud and cybersecurity solutions engineering. Twenty years spent training new personnel, briefing senior leadership, and coordinating large-scale operations built the same skill a solutions engineer uses every day, translating complex technical material for the person in front of you. Recent, concentrated technical study, two Vets-to-Tech certificate programs, an AWS Cloud Practitioner certification, CompTIA Network+, and an active Secret-level security clearance, gives that communication foundation a technical base to stand on.
honestPositioningNote: This is a transition, not a lateral move. Tim has not held a quota-carrying sales or SE title. What transfers directly is technical communication under pressure: presenting to audiences with widely differing levels of preparation, one-on-one technical troubleshooting, and curriculum/documentation for non-expert audiences. Any application built from this profile should lead with that transfer, not imply prior SE tenure.

technicalSkills.cloud_and_security: AWS EC2 deployment, AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner, security hardening, vulnerability assessment, threat intelligence, MFA/TLS implementation, Azure (limited exposure via WaV2T)
technicalSkills.systems_and_networking: Windows Server/PowerShell, Linux/Bash, Active Directory & Group Policy, TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, IPv4/IPv6, subnetting, VPN, network troubleshooting
technicalSkills.automation: Python scripting (~1 year hands-on), Ansible infrastructure automation, Vagrant (project-based, single-VM provisioning), C# (fundamentals)
technicalSkills.virtualization: VirtualBox (multi-VM AD network simulation, hosted Kali Linux, Linux administration coursework)
technicalSkills.communication_and_translation: Technical documentation for non-expert audiences, curriculum design and delivery, live technical instruction to groups ranging from new trainees to senior leaders, structured Q&A/briefing under time pressure

certifications.name: CompTIA Security+
certifications.note: Exam scheduled July 30, 2026, McChord testing center, 12:30pm. Satisfies DoD 8570/8140 IAT Level II upon passing.

experience.title: Information Services Intern
bullets: Communicated technical requirements and solutions to non-technical agency staff, translating IT concepts for end users across account, software, and workstation issues.
Logged and tracked service requests in ServiceNow within a strict data privacy and compliance environment.
Diagnosed and resolved three network connectivity failures by tracing physical connections through patch panels and verifying switch port assignments, mixed supervised and independent work.

experience.title: IT Trainee — Cybersecurity
bullets: Completed a 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science, with a 100% lab completion rate across security hardening, threat intelligence, vulnerability assessment, and cloud technologies.
Hosted a Kali Linux virtual machine in VirtualBox for hands-on cybersecurity exploration exercises.

experience.title: IT Trainee — Server & Cloud Applications
bullets: Completed a 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science, with a 100% lab completion rate across Linux, Windows Server, networking, AWS, and Python scripting.

Built a multi-VM VirtualBox network with Active Directory to simulate a Windows Server/sysadmin environment; solo capstone project provisioned a single VM using Vagrant with an Ansible playbook customized from an instructor-provided template.

experience.title: Production Manager / Library, Security & Operations Supervisor
bullets: Sustained 100% readiness across 400-plus public events and an audience reach of 2.5 million-plus, coordinating logistics and a large equipment inventory.
Delivered resilience and performance training to 200 personnel across 10 cohorts as a certified Level 2 facilitator.
Administered personnel security clearance records for 40-plus team members.

experience.title: Lead Instructor / Curriculum Writer
bullets: Instructed 100-plus new trainees and advised 20 mid-level and 40 senior leaders, assessing 400 student performances across audiences with widely differing levels of preparation.
Contributed as part of a curriculum team to a 58% reduction in training cycle time.
Achieved a 100% student pass rate against the program's full testing standard; awarded the Distinguished Leader Award, Army Leadership Development Program, 2010.

education.degree: Cybersecurity Certificate (18 credits, B.S. Computer Science)
education.institution: Washington Vets-to-Tech / Saint Martin's University, Lacey, WA

University of Iowa, Iowa City, IA

projects.name: SIP — Systematic Instrumental Practice
projects.description: In-progress Android-first Flutter application for instrumental musicians using FSRS-5 scheduling, a Python/FastAPI backend, and py-fsrs. Grounded in doctoral research applying Edwin Gordon's Music Learning Theory. A multi-model AI design council (Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, Perplexity) produced the canonical design specification.
projects.relevance: Demonstrates product thinking, system design, and the ability to translate a domain problem into a technical solution, evidence of the same translation skill an SE applies to a customer's problem, not a claim of shipped commercial software.

projects.name: Vagrant + Ansible VM Provisioning (WaV2T Capstone)
projects.description: Solo capstone project provisioning a single VM via Vagrant, using an Ansible playbook customized from an instructor-provided template.
projects.relevance: Concrete, named project anchor for infrastructure-as-code exposure.

additional: Maintains website, CMS, and social media (WordPress, Canva, Bluehost, Google Forms) and runs live sound board operations for a community theatre organization, ongoing
